personality_one_liner: A loud, high-octane competitive sports arcade game driven by punchy typography, deep obsidian surfaces, and blistering warm accents.

spacing.rule: Use tight spacing for metrics and dense gameplay HUDs. Provide generous padding for primary CTAs like 'Play' and the 'Tums' button. Ensure bottom safe-area clearance for sticky actions.

do's: Use solid surfaceSecondary or border dividers to define hierarchy, keeping the sports-arcade vibe fast and clean without performance drops from blur.
dont's: Avoid glassmorphism. This is a fast-paced game; blur layers cause performance drops and conflict with the raw, high-contrast utility aesthetic.

screens.name: Home Lobby
screens.purpose: The main entry point driving players immediately into the next contest while teasing rank and daily tasks.

states.loading: Centered loading spinner with 'Warming up the grill...' text.
states.empty: N/A - Player stats and play button are always present.
states.error: Failed to load player profile. 'Check connection' with a Retry button.

screens.layout: Top bar: Player avatar (left), Coin/Tums balances (right). Center: Massive 'Play Next Contest' hero card featuring food imagery, entry fee, and a bold brandPrimary CTA. Below Hero: A progress strip showing global rank or win streak. Bottom: Daily challenge chips stacked in a 2-column grid.

screens.name: Contest Selection
screens.purpose: A high-energy carousel of real-world inspired eating contests (Hot Dogs, Wings, Pizza).

states.empty: No contests available right now. Check back later.
states.error: Unable to fetch live contests. Tap to refresh.

screens.layout: Horizontal snapping carousel. Each card uses a tinted background image of the food (with gradient scrim), bold display titles, entry fee (coins), prize pool, and difficulty indicator. A sticky 'Enter Contest' button anchors the bottom.

screens.name: Live Gameplay
screens.purpose: The rapid tap-to-eat screen balancing speed with the Heartburn penalty.

states.error: Connection lost to opponent. Forfeit or Retry.

screens.layout: Top: Score/Timer row and opponent progress bars racing horizontally. Left edge: Vertical Heartburn Meter (gauge filling from bottom to top, shifting green to red). Center: Massive interactive tap zone with the food item. Bottom Right: Oversized, tactile 'TUMS' pill button (brandPrimary) to instantly reduce heartburn. Trash talk speech bubbles appear dynamically over opponent avatars.

screens.name: Leaderboard
screens.purpose: Showcase global and local eating champions.

states.empty: No rankings yet. Play a match to place!
states.error: Unable to load leaderboard. Tap to retry.

screens.layout: Sticky header with Segmented control (Global / Friends). Body is a flat list. Each row displays rank number (Bebas Neue, large), country flag, Avatar, Username, and Total Food Consumed. Top 3 rows are emphasized with surfaceSecondary backgrounds and brandSecondary (gold) accents.

screens.name: Shop
screens.purpose: Store for buying Tums packs and Coins via virtual or real currency.
screens.layout: 2-column grid of store items. Each item card has a surfaceSecondary background, an illustration/icon (stack of coins, bottle of pills), quantity, and price button. Daily free reward card pinned to the top.

nathans_hotdogs_bg.alt: Three hotdogs held in hands representing the hot dog eating contest

wing_bowl_bg.alt: Spicy fried chicken wings on a plate

stuffed_crust_pizza_bg.alt: Stuffed crust pizza being pulled with melted cheese

instructions_to_main_agent: Use Personality 7 (Dark-First Utility DARK) combined with high-contrast, arcade-sports elements. The app needs to feel fast, competitive, and loud.
Implement Bebas Neue for all display headings, scores, counters, and metrics. Use DM Sans strictly for body copy, labels, and secondary metadata.
Ensure the Live Gameplay screen prioritizes tap responsiveness. The Heartburn Meter should be a vertical gauge filling from bottom to top.
The 'Tums' button must be an oversized, tactile button anchored on the screen for immediate access. Use 'brandPrimary' (Hot Sauce Red) to make it punchy.
Do NOT use glassmorphism or blur effects. Keep the UI flat, sharp, and high-performance utilizing solid 'surfaceSecondary' for cards and overlays.
Background imagery on Contest Selection cards MUST use the mandatory gradient-to-dark scrim pattern (top-down, transparent to >=70% surfaceInverse) to ensure text remains highly readable.
Haptics: Wire strong impact haptics to the tap-to-eat interactions and Tums consumption. Include a `haptics` events map in the code.
